confirmed.title: PHP workflow SKILL lists WorkflowVisualizer React component as pending, but it is fully shipped
confirmed.evidence: SKILL.md line 54 sits under the '### Pendente' header (line 51) and states: '- **WorkflowVisualizer React component** — diagrama interativo do workflow (states + transitions) consumindo WorkflowDefinition::toArray().' However, the component is fully implemented and exported: packages-js/workflow/src/index.ts exports `WorkflowVisualizer`, `buildMermaidSource`, `slugifyStateId` from './WorkflowVisualizer.js'; the implementation is packages-js/workflow/src/WorkflowVisualizer.tsx (205 lines) with a 115-line test file WorkflowVisualizer.test.tsx, delivered in commit a5c60e1 (feat(workflow): add WorkflowVisualizer Mermaid graph React component). The sibling packages-js/workflow/SKILL.md documents it as delivered under 'WorkflowVisualizer (WF-005)' (lines 88-138), and this same PHP SKILL's own Related section references it. The item is incorrectly marked as 'Por chegar'/pending.
confirmed.fix: Remove the WorkflowVisualizer bullet from the '### Pendente' section and record WF-005 as delivered (as the JS workflow SKILL already does), or move it to the delivered/status area noting it ships in @arqel-dev/workflow.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED. packages/workflow/SKILL.md line 54 sits under the '### Pendente' header (line 51) and lists '**WorkflowVisualizer React component** — diagrama interativo do workflow (states + transitions) consumindo WorkflowDefinition::toArray().' as unshipped. But the component is fully delivered: packages-js/workflow/src/index.ts exports `WorkflowVisualizer`, `buildMermaidSource`, `slugifyStateId` from './WorkflowVisualizer.js'; the implementation is packages-js/workflow/src/WorkflowVisualizer.tsx (205 lines) with a companion WorkflowVisualizer.test.tsx (115 lines). The sibling packages-js/workflow/SKILL.md documents it as delivered under '## WorkflowVisualizer (WF-005)' (line 88) with runnable usage examples (lines 97-123). This is a real, new, actionable contradiction: the PHP SKILL marks a fully-shipped component as pending. It is NOT a swept family — workflow is explicitly listed as still-unswept, and the only prior workflow SWEPT item was the versioning package's <VersionDiff>, a different component. The fix: remove the WorkflowVisualizer bullet from '### Pendente' and record WF-005 as delivered (shipping in @arqel-dev/workflow), matching the JS SKILL.

confirmed.title: PT-BR page cross-links to English locale (missing /pt-BR/ prefix) — 3 broken locale references
confirmed.evidence: In apps/docs/pt-BR/guide/tutorial-first-crud.md three internal links drop the /pt-BR/ locale prefix and point at the English pages instead of their PT-BR mirrors: line 5 `[Getting Started](/guide/getting-started)`, line 80 `[Getting Started](/guide/getting-started)`, and line 310 `[PHP overview](/reference/php-overview)`. The PT-BR targets exist (apps/docs/pt-BR/guide/getting-started.md and apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php-overview.md), and the ES mirror apps/docs/es/guide/tutorial-first-crud.md correctly uses /es/guide/getting-started (lines 5, 80) and /es/reference/php-overview (line 310). The same PT-BR file even links correctly elsewhere (lines 306-307 use /pt-BR/advanced/custom-fields and /pt-BR/advanced/macros), confirming the un-prefixed links are a defect, not a convention. Effect: a PT-BR reader following these links is silently dropped onto the English pages, breaking the localized navigation.
confirmed.fix: Change the three links to their PT-BR mirrors: line 5 and line 80 `(/guide/getting-started)` -> `(/pt-BR/guide/getting-started)`; line 310 `(/reference/php-overview)` -> `(/pt-BR/reference/php-overview)`, matching the ES mirror and the file's own /pt-BR/ links.

verdict.reason: Independently confirmed by opening apps/docs/pt-BR/guide/tutorial-first-crud.md. Lines 5 and 80 both contain [Getting Started](/guide/getting-started) and line 310 contains [PHP overview](/reference/php-overview) — all three drop the /pt-BR/ locale prefix and silently route a PT-BR reader to the English pages. The PT-BR mirror targets exist (apps/docs/pt-BR/guide/getting-started.md and apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php-overview.md). The ES mirror (apps/docs/es/guide/tutorial-first-crud.md) correctly prefixes the same three links (/es/guide/getting-started lines 5 & 80, /es/reference/php-overview line 310), and the same PT-BR file's own lines 308-309 correctly use /pt-BR/advanced/custom-fields and /pt-BR/advanced/macros — proving the un-prefixed links are a defect, not a convention. This is a NEW, distinct file+line contradiction: the SWEPT "trilingual mirror drift" family covers API/content drift (signatures, params, delivered features) between mirrors, not intra-page broken locale-prefix links. Fix: prefix the three links with /pt-BR/.
